answerScope: Farnsworth Court and Greenwich Millennium Village evidence, primarily GMV Phases 1 and 2 where the documents are specific; later GMV phases are noted only where explicitly stated.

mongoSearch.note: MongoDB authentication succeeded after the key was corrected. Atlas Vector Search and supporting lookup/text indexes were created on agent_memory.documents. The existing answer values were already consistent with matching retrieved chunks from the same archive.

developmentName.value: Farnsworth Court / Greenwich Millennium Village Phases 1 and 2

evidence.snippet: The due-diligence report is specifically about Farnsworth Court and repeatedly discusses GMV Phases 1 and 2 evidence.
No public proof that Farnsworth has MVHR; the fire statement instead describes mechanical or natural smoke ventilation to protected corridors, which is a different thing.
Protected corridors have a means of smoke ventilation, mechanical or natural. This is smoke-control/fire strategy evidence, not dwelling ventilation type.
No verified Farnsworth-specific active cooling system was found in the public material reviewed.
For later phases, cooling demand was to be minimised through solar shading; this does not prove Farnsworth Court has active cooling.
Farnsworth listing evidence refers to a secure parking space on the ground floor of a car park. No evidence found that the car park is heated.
GMV design summary says car parking was restricted and sited away from properties.
Evidence points to community heating and hot water, not individual in-flat boilers; EPC-derived records describe community-scheme heating and hot water.
HouseMetric/EPC summary says heating is a community scheme with CHP.

gasSupply.interpretation: Yes at estate/energy-centre level for the CHP/community heat network; no evidence found of individual flat gas boilers or direct individual gas supply.

evidence.snippet: EPC-derived records describe CHP/community mains gas as the main fuel arrangement.
Later-phase planning report refers to gas-fired CHP units as lead heat source for the site heat network.
No reliable 60A/100A flat supply amperage evidence was found.
No ADSL/FTTC/FTTH/FTTP evidence specific to Farnsworth Court was found.

liftsPerTower.interpretation: At least one lift is evidenced; exact number per tower/core is not evidenced.

evidence.snippet: The apartment is described as being in a lift-serviced block; building features include lift.

liftSizeOrCargoElevator.value: Unknown; no cargo/service elevator evidence found. Fire statement says evacuation lifts were not considered viable as part of remedial works.

evidence.snippet: Inclusion of evacuation lifts was not considered a reasonable or viable option as part of external wall remedial works.
No roof terrace evidence found; listings refer to balcony and landscaped communal gardens instead.
No barbeque/barbecue facility evidence found.

cycleParking.interpretation: GMV development-level evidence says secure bicycle storage; exact Farnsworth location/security arrangement not confirmed.

evidence.snippet: Environmental design summary includes secure bicycle storage.
Planning transport discussion refers to improved cycle parking and potential redesign of car parking spaces as cycle parking facilities for later phases.
No 1-5 cycle parking security rating evidence found.
Building features include 24-hour concierge/security and the development is described as portered.
Companies House correspondence address includes The Concierge Office, Metcalfe Court.
No gym evidence found.
No sauna evidence found.
No swimming-pool evidence found.
No sunbed evidence found.

evidence.source: Findings-gmv/Greenwich Millennium Village legal and dispute map.pdf
evidence.snippet: The legal/dispute report says no GMV-specific published court, tribunal or ombudsman decision centred on mould was found.
The report says long-term condensation performance remains unresolved because public material is weak on vapour control, thermal bridges, and related as-built details.

leakingPipes.interpretation: GMV-level evidence of catastrophic flooding from cold-water-system failures; not proven specifically for Farnsworth Court.

evidence.snippet: GMV brought claims for catastrophic flooding caused by failures in a building cold water system, including metallic debris, over-tightening of a nut, a non-return valve where none belonged, and a closed isolation valve.
No leaking-roof evidence found.
No blocked-pipe evidence found; the strongest water-services evidence concerns flooding from cold-water-system failures.

incidents.description: Inside Housing reported Holly Court lift problems: a resident said she had to carry her seven-year-old daughter with cerebral palsy to a fourth-floor flat because of problems with the lift. This is GMV/Holly Court evidence, not Farnsworth-specific.

evidence.snippet: Article date 09.07.07; Hoa Luu had been forced to carry her seven-year-old daughter up to her fourth-floor flat because of problems with the lift.

incidents.description: Not a roof leak: included as nearest available schema category for a GMV catastrophic flooding/water-system defect litigated in 2013. The source describes cold-water-system failures causing ?4.75m damage. Treat as water-services/flooding evidence, not roof evidence.

evidence.snippet: Catastrophic flooding occurred at a residential development owned by GMV, caused by failures in the building cold water system, with ?4.75 million damage.

incidents.description: Fire-safety/remediation trigger rather than an actual fire incident: after Government EWS guidance, GMVML commissioned intrusive surveys and all 10 high-rise buildings were deemed to require EWS remediation, including replacement of cladding and installation of suitable fire breaks where missing or inadequate.

evidence.snippet: Following 20 January 2020 guidance, GMVML commissioned EWS surveys; all 10 high-rise buildings required remediation and suitable fire breaks where missing or inadequate.

incidents.description: Not a dated outage: Switch2 reported the existing heat network serving phases 1 and 2 was outdated and difficult to maintain, and announced a 30-year retrofit/operation/maintenance partnership intended to upgrade equipment while maintaining heat and hot water supply.

evidence.snippet: Publication date Monday 1 August 2022; existing heat network serves phases 1 and 2, originally built between 2000 and 2009, with plant and equipment described as out-dated and difficult to maintain.

notFoundInReviewedSources: Broadband type: ADSL, FTTC, FTTH or FTTP
Exact number of lifts per tower/core
